data_website_builder_workflow.overview: This mode builds data-driven websites by combining user-provided templates with dataset analysis and visualization. The workflow integrates FastAPI backends, Tailwind CSS styling, Chart.js visualizations, and Carbon Design System principles.

actions.action: Review the website template provided by the user
Identify all pages and their purposes
Note any specific design elements or layouts

For each page in the template, ask the user to describe: - The page's purpose and target audience - What data should be displayed - What types of visualizations are needed (tables, charts, graphs) - Any interactive features required - Specific layout or design preferences
Ask the user to provide base styling files (base.css, styles.css, etc.)
If no styling file is provided, confirm use of Tailwind CSS with Carbon Design System principles
Note any specific color schemes, fonts, or branding requirements
Use the Dataset MCP server tools
Review the list of available datasets and their descriptions
Identify which datasets are relevant to the project requirements

step.title: Perform Exploratory Data Analysis

actions.action: For each relevant dataset, use run_pandas_code to: - Load the dataset - Examine the structure (df.info(), df.describe()) - Check for missing values - Identify key columns and data types - Calculate summary statistics - Identify interesting patterns or insights
Document findings that will inform visualization choices

step.tool: use the Pandas Dataset Code Execution tool

actions.action: Based on EDA findings, determine appropriate chart types: - Line charts for trends over time - Bar charts for comparisons - Pie/donut charts for proportions - Scatter plots for correlations - Tables for detailed data display
Plan which data aggregations or transformations are needed
Consider accessibility and readability of visualizations

step.title: Create FastAPI Backend Structure

actions.action: Create main FastAPI application file (main.py or app.py)
Set up CORS middleware for frontend communication
Create data processing utilities
Organize code with proper separation of concerns

step.file_structure: backend/ ├── main.py # FastAPI application ├── data_processing.py # Data transformation functions ├── models.py # Pydantic models for API responses └── requirements.txt # Python dependencies

actions.action: Build HTML pages based on the template
Include proper semantic HTML5 elements
Add meta tags for responsiveness and SEO
Link Tailwind CSS and Chart.js libraries
Structure content with accessibility in mind
Apply Tailwind utility classes for layout and styling
Use responsive design classes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:)
Incorporate user-provided base.css if available
Follow Carbon Design System color and spacing principles
Ensure consistent spacing and typography
Create canvas elements for each chart
Use static data provided earlier from the Pandas Dataset tools
Do not attempt to request any data dynamically via APIs
Configure Chart.js with appropriate options: - Responsive: true - Accessible labels and legends - Appropriate colors following design system - Interactive tooltips
Handle loading states and errors gracefully
Build responsive HTML tables for detailed data display
Apply Tailwind classes for styling
Consider pagination for large datasets
Add sorting and filtering if appropriate
Ensure tables are accessible with proper headers
Use carbon-mcp server to search for relevant design patterns
Apply Carbon color tokens and spacing guidelines
Follow Carbon typography recommendations
Ensure accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA)
Start the FastAPI server
Open the frontend in a browser
Verify each page loads correctly
Write README.md with: - Project overview - Setup instructions - How to run the application - API endpoint documentation - Technology stack details
Add inline code comments for complex logic
Document any configuration options

completion_criteria.criterion: All pages from the template are implemented
FastAPI backend serves all required data endpoints
Responsive design works on mobile, tablet, and desktop
Styling follows user preferences and Carbon principles
